summary: Alfred has a solid CI/CD foundation with GitHub Actions covering check, test, format, and lint stages. Cargo.lock ensures build reproducibility. E2E testing via Docker is configured. Areas for improvement: no branch protection rules, no secret management formalization, and single-stage Docker for e2e (not production-ready).

recommendations.title: Enable branch protection on main
recommendations.description: Configure GitHub branch protection to require CI checks (check, test, format, lint, e2e) before merge. Optionally require 1-2 reviews for production readiness.

recommendations.title: Add Docker multi-stage build for e2e tests
recommendations.description: Split e2e Dockerfile into builder and runtime stages to reduce image size and surface area. Remove Rust toolchain and build tools from runtime stage.

recommendations.title: Add secret scanning and rotation policy
recommendations.description: Enable GitHub secret scanning to detect hardcoded secrets. Document policy for handling any discovered credentials. Consider using GitHub Secrets for sensitive values.

recommendations.title: Add failure notifications to CI
recommendations.description: Configure email or webhook notifications for CI failures to ensure timely response. Consider Slack or similar for team-wide alerts.

recommendations.title: Create production deployment pipeline
recommendations.description: Once Alfred reaches v1.0, add deployment stage with canary or blue-green strategy. Consider using act or similar for local CI testing.

recommendations.title: Add build matrix for multi-platform testing
recommendations.description: Extend CI to test on macOS (apple-silicon) and Windows runners in addition to ubuntu-latest. Ensures cross-platform compatibility.

recommendations.title: Run pre-commit hook checks in CI
recommendations.description: CI currently duplicates pre-commit hook logic (format, lint, test). Consider extracting shared logic or ensuring CI mirrors local hook behavior exactly.
